tokenGroup.description: The full range of color options in the system. Colors are organized into named hue families, each with a grade scale from 0 (lightest) to 900 (darkest). The 450 grades are reserved for brand usage.

children.description: The red hue family. Pushpin 450 is the system's primary hero color, used for brand moments and primary product actions.
Lightest tint of the Pushpin red family. Suitable for large background surfaces and subtle error-state backgrounds.

agentDocumentBlocks.purpose: Apply the primary text color for body content, headings, and labels on default background surfaces.

items.description: Use primary for default reading content; use secondary for supporting or reduced-emphasis text.
Use primary on default surfaces; use on-action inside filled interactive components.

items.guidance: Do not use on dark or colored background surfaces.
Do not override this token value at the component level.

children.description: Very light tint of the Pushpin red family.

children.description: The system's primary hero color. Reserved for brand usage and primary product actions. Among the least accessible colors in the palette — do not use for functional color pairings without explicit contrast verification.

items.guidance: Reserve this color for brand moments and the primary product action. Do not use for general UI elements.
items.rationale: Pushpin 450 is the system's hero color. Overusing it dilutes its impact and creates accessibility challenges, as the 450 grades have limited contrast pairing options.

children.description: The neutral gray family. The foundation of the system's layering model and the most commonly used color family in both light and dark modes.

children.description: Mid-gray. Used for subtle text, default borders, and disabled states in light mode. The minimum gray that meets WCAG AA contrast on white for large text.

children.description: The near-black value. Used as the default text color and background surface in dark mode.
Near-black. The darkest color in the system — used for default text in light mode and the default background in dark mode.

items.title: Intent
items.body: Define and organize a set of related design tokens, providing shared defaults for type and category.

items.guidance: Do not reference base palette tokens directly in component code.
items.rationale: Always reference semantic tokens that point to palette values.

items.guidance: The 450 colors are reserved for brand usage. Do not use 450 grades for functional UI elements without explicit accessibility verification.
items.rationale: The 450 grades are among the least accessible colors in the palette. They work best within larger brand moments and are not suitable for functional color pairings.

items.guidance: Use the darkGray text color on any background color at grade 400 or below. Use white text on grade 500 and above.
items.rationale: Ensures WCAG 2.1 AA contrast compliance for text placed on palette colors.

items.guidance: Do not use base palette tokens directly in product code. Use semantic tokens that reference these base values instead.
items.rationale: Semantic tokens adapt automatically across color modes. Direct use of base palette tokens bypasses the theming system and will produce incorrect results in dark mode.

items.title: Intent
items.body: Define and organize a set of related design tokens, providing shared defaults for type and category.

items.guidance: Do not reference base palette tokens directly in component code.
items.rationale: Always reference semantic tokens that point to palette values.
